sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Stephanie Morales is a 36-year-old female presenting with ankle pain after a fall two days ago.
She reports that she was walking to her car and slipped on ice, twisting her left ankle on the outside.
She fell, but was able to get up with assistance.
She describes the pain as severe, with swelling and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation with some relief.
Past medical history: She has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
Review of systems: No additional symptoms in other organs are reported.
Current medications:
Metformin for diabetes, Norvasc for high blood pressure.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70 mmHg.
Physical exam: The neck exam is normal, with no bruits.
The lungs are clear, and the heart exam is normal, with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that is unchanged from previous exams.
The belly exam is normal.
The ankle exam on the left shows tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the 5th metatarsal, with swelling and redness.
There is pain with valgus stressing of the ankle, but normal sensation and pulses.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect an ankle sprain, but would like to order an x-ray to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; Diabetes; Hypertension.
Plan: I will order an x-ray of the ankle, and have the patient use crutches to avoid weightbearing until the results are available.
I will give the patient naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control, and instruct her to keep the ankle elevated and apply ice.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
I will also refill her Norvasc, and order a hemoglobin A1c and baseline labs for her diabetes management.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient to discuss the x-ray results and adjust her plan as needed.
